title: Reviving Mexican Rice Tamales
description: Fermented rice noodle tamales with a cheese pull, perfect for an energizing meal.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons vegetable oil in a pan and sauté diced onion and minced garlic until translucent.
Add 150g rice stick noodles to the pan and stir for 2 minutes to lightly toast them.
In a bowl, whisk 1 cup masa harina with 1 teaspoon salt and 1 tablespoon lime juice until a smooth dough forms.
Use a mortar and pestle to pound 1 tablespoon fermented bean paste into a smooth paste.
Fold the sautéed noodle mixture into the masa dough, then fold in the pounded bean paste and 1 cup shredded cheese.
Spread the dough evenly onto soaked corn husks, leaving a margin; fold the husks into tamale shape.
Steam the assembled tamales over high heat for 30 minutes or until the masa is firm and the cheese is melted and pulled.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh cilantro and lime wedges.
